title: santolina pallida
intro: Santolina pallida, also known as Grey Cotton Lavender, is a low-growing, shrubby perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its essential oil composition. The health benefits of Santolina pallida include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Santolina pallida can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, and salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be effectively harnessed.
